mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of months with rising PAYEMS, or rank correlation between PAYEMS change and forward returns is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy suppresses equity risk appetite and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as liquidity tightening propagates through credit markets.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales and industrial production captures a broad demand-supply coordination failure that foreshadows negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where both RSAFS and INDPRO decline jointly, S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during a monetary tightening cycle signals a hard-landing scenario where corporate earnings expectations collapse, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp during tightening cycles, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the conditional return spread versus non-tightening regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals a positive productivity shock that lifts near-term corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of weeks when INDPRO accelerates, or the rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day returns is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in new private housing starts signals deteriorating construction activity and negative household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls more than 5% over 3 months, S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the signal hit rate is not significantly below 50% (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high consumer price inflation forces a repricing of real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to inflation surprises.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%, or the rank correlation between inflation surprises and 5-day returns is positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend validates aggregate demand strength and supports upward earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with above-trend GDPC1 growth, or the rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns is negative over a 3-year window.
